issue.title: Use generated request adapter for WP_Http::request redirect options

artifacts.role: WPHX-312.69 runner installing compiler-emitted class-wp-http.php request shell
typed public shell declaration with named redirectOptions helper metadata
WordPress-profile adapter IR for request redirect option support
redirect-options candidate manifest with generated-shell and IR feature evidence
ownership manifest marking the boundary as compiler_emitted_original_path_shell
behavior receipt for the redirect-options candidate

claims: WPHX-312.69 now consumes the compiler-emitted original-path WP_Http::request shell instead of a copied/runner-patched class-wp-http.php body.
The redirect-options helper remains the bounded Haxe-owned branch claim, with helper modules compiled for the generated shell's runtime calls.
The shared generated request shell now calls the named redirectOptions helper for the zero-redirection follow_redirects decision.
Both deterministic redirect-options oracle/candidate probes remain behavior-identical.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership.
This does not claim redirect following, redirect count filtering semantics, live network behavior, upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity, or installed distribution behavior.
This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
